decisions.title: Phoenix Project V1 MVP Deadline
decisions.detail: The main goal for V1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st.

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: V1 Deadline is Hard Constraint
decisions.detail: The September 1st deadline for V1 is a hard constraint due to an upcoming marketing campaign launch on September 5th.

evidence.quote: We have a hard constraint on the deadline because of the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Marketing Ads Booked
decisions.detail: Marketing advertisements for the project have already been booked, reinforcing the V1 deadline.

evidence.quote: Yes, we've already booked the ads.

decisions.title: V1 Must Include User Authentication
decisions.detail: The V1 MVP must include user authentication functionality.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication

decisions.title: V1 Must Include Project Dashboard
decisions.detail: The V1 MVP must include a working project dashboard.

evidence.quote: and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: System Performance Requirement: Super Fast
decisions.detail: The system must be 'super fast'.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure it's super fast.

decisions.title: Frontend Technology: React
decisions.detail: React will be used for the frontend development.

evidence.quote: we are using React for the frontend

decisions.title: Backend Technology: Python (FastAPI)
decisions.detail: Python with FastAPI will be used for the backend service.

evidence.quote: and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Rationale for Python: Existing Libraries
decisions.detail: The decision to use Python is based on existing team libraries, which makes it a suitable choice.

evidence.quote: I think it makes the most sense for our team since we already have libraries built in Python.

decisions.title: Database Choice Question
decisions.detail: The choice for the project's database was initially an open question.

evidence.quote: What about the database?

decisions.title: Database Technology: PostgreSQL
decisions.detail: PostgreSQL will be used as the database for the project.

evidence.quote: We'll use PostgreSQL.

decisions.title: Database Model: Standard Relational
decisions.detail: A standard relational model will be used for the database.

evidence.quote: Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Session Management Approach Question
decisions.detail: The approach for session management (Redis vs. standard database sessions) was an open question.

evidence.quote: But wait, what about session management? Should we use Redis or just standard database sessions?

decisions.title: Redis for Session Management Deferred to V2
decisions.detail: The use of Redis for session management will be deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker.

evidence.quote: Let's defer Redis to V2 unless it's a performance blocker.

decisions.title: V1 Session Management: Standard PostgreSQL Sessions
decisions.detail: For V1, standard PostgreSQL sessions will be used for session management.

evidence.quote: Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now to save budget.

decisions.title: Rationale for PostgreSQL Sessions: Budget Saving
decisions.detail: The decision to use standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 is to save budget.

evidence.quote: to save budget.

decisions.title: User Authentication Implementation Method Question
decisions.detail: The method for implementing user authentication was an open question.

evidence.quote: How are we doing user auth?

decisions.title: User Authentication Should Not Be Built In-House
decisions.detail: The team should not build user authentication functionality themselves.

evidence.quote: We don't want to build it ourselves, right?

decisions.title: V1 User Authentication Service: Firebase Authentication
decisions.detail: For V1, Firebase Authentication will be integrated for user authentication.

evidence.quote: for V1, let's just integrate Firebase Authentication.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Benefits
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication was chosen because it's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches pricing requirements.

evidence.quote: It's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches our pricing requirements.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Login Method: Google Sign-in
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow Google sign-in.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Login Method: Email Logins
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow email logins.

evidence.quote: and email logins.

decisions.title: User Registration Acceptance Criteria Question
decisions.detail: There was an open question regarding the acceptance criteria for user registration.

evidence.quote: Do we have acceptance criteria for user registration?

decisions.title: User Registration Fields Question
decisions.detail: There was an open question regarding what fields to collect during user registration.

evidence.quote: What fields do we collect?

decisions.title: User Registration Field: Email
decisions.detail: Email is a required field for user registration.

evidence.quote: Just email

decisions.title: User Registration Field: Full Name
decisions.detail: Full name is a required field for user registration.

evidence.quote: full name

decisions.title: User Registration Field: Role
decisions.detail: Role is a required field for user registration.

evidence.quote: and role.

decisions.title: No Address or Telephone Number for User Registration (for now)
decisions.detail: Address and telephone number are not required fields for user registration at this time.

evidence.quote: We don't need address or telephone number for now.

decisions.title: User Registration Field: Company Name (B2B Requirement)
decisions.detail: Company name must be collected during user registration because the project is B2B.

evidence.quote: Wait, we need to collect company name too! We are B2B.

decisions.title: Company Name is Required During Signup
decisions.detail: The company name field is explicitly required during the user signup process.

evidence.quote: Company name is required during signup.

decisions.title: Deployment Strategy Question
decisions.detail: The overall deployment strategy for the project was an open question.

evidence.quote: What about deployment?

decisions.title: Backend Deployment: Cloud Run for FastAPI
decisions.detail: Cloud Run is the chosen deployment platform for the FastAPI backend application.

evidence.quote: I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app

decisions.title: Frontend Deployment: Vercel for React App
decisions.detail: Vercel is the chosen deployment platform for the React frontend application.

evidence.quote: and Vercel for the React app.

decisions.title: Vercel Deployment for Frontend is Acceptable
decisions.detail: Deployment of the frontend React app on Vercel is acceptable.

evidence.quote: Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Security Concern with Cloud Run Backend
decisions.detail: A security concern was raised regarding the use of Cloud Run for the backend.

evidence.quote: but for the backend, is Cloud Run secure?

decisions.title: Database Placement: Private VPC
decisions.detail: The database must be placed in a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

evidence.quote: we will place the database in a private VPC
evidence.approximate_location: John's statement on Cloud Run security

decisions.title: Cloud Run Connection to DB: Serverless VPC Access
decisions.detail: Cloud Run must connect to the database via Serverless VPC Access.

evidence.quote: and connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.
evidence.approximate_location: John's statement on Cloud Run security

decisions.title: PostgreSQL Instance Not Exposed to Public Internet
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet.

evidence.quote: That way the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet.
evidence.approximate_location: John's statement on Cloud Run security

decisions.title: Backend Security as a Strict Technical Constraint
decisions.detail: The security of the backend deployment is considered a strict technical constraint.

evidence.quote: This is a strict technical constraint for security.
evidence.approximate_location: John's statement on Cloud Run security

decisions.title: Auth Flow Requires Thorough Testing
decisions.detail: The authentication flow needs to be tested thoroughly.

evidence.quote: We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.

decisions.title: Session Token Expiration Duration Question
decisions.detail: The duration for session token expiration was an open question.

evidence.quote: If a user logs in, how long does the session last?

decisions.title: Session Token Expiration: 24 Hours (Adjustable)
decisions.detail: The session token expiration is set to 24 hours for now, with the possibility to adjust it later.

evidence.quote: Let's set token expiration to 24 hours. We can adjust it later.

decisions.title: Dashboard Metrics to Display Question
decisions.detail: There was an open question regarding what metrics should be displayed on the dashboard.

evidence.quote: What metrics are we displaying?

decisions.title: Dashboard Metric: Active User Metrics
decisions.detail: The dashboard must display active user metrics.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics

decisions.title: Dashboard Metric: System Status
decisions.detail: The dashboard must display system status.

evidence.quote: system status

decisions.title: Dashboard Metric: Project Lists
decisions.detail: The dashboard must display project lists.

evidence.quote: and project lists.

decisions.title: Dashboard Features to be Detailed in Next Sync
decisions.detail: The detailed features of the dashboard will be discussed and specified in the next synchronization meeting.

evidence.quote: Let's detail the dashboard features in the next sync.

decisions.title: Responsibility for Firebase Provisioning Question
decisions.detail: The responsibility for handling Firebase provisioning was an open question.

evidence.quote: who is handling the Firebase provisioning?

decisions.title: Firebase Config Keys Needed by Next Week
decisions.detail: The configuration keys for Firebase are needed by next week.

evidence.quote: I need the config keys by next week.

decisions.title: Mike Assigned Firebase Provisioning
decisions.detail: Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning.

evidence.quote: I'll assign that to Mike.

decisions.title: Firebase Config Keys Due by July 8th
decisions.detail: The Firebase configuration keys should be ready by July 8th.

evidence.quote: He should have it ready by July 8th.

decisions.title: Sync with Marketing on Dashboard Design Next Wednesday
decisions.detail: There is a dependency to sync with the marketing team regarding the dashboard design next Wednesday.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

implementation_scope: Integrate Firebase Authentication for user authentication
Configure Firebase to allow Google Sign-in
Configure Firebase to allow email logins
Implement user registration with required fields: Email, Full Name, Role, Company Name
Implement standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 session management
Develop backend service using Python with FastAPI
Configure Cloud Run for FastAPI backend deployment
Configure Vercel for React frontend deployment
Implement thorough testing for authentication flows
Implement dashboard display for active user metrics, system status, and project lists
technical_constraints: Must leverage existing team libraries in Python
Database technology: PostgreSQL with a standard relational model
Redis for session management is deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker
User authentication functionality must not be built in-house
Database must be placed in a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)
Cloud Run must connect to the database via Serverless VPC Access
PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet
Backend security is a strict technical constraint
Firebase configuration keys needed by July 8th
open_technical_questions: What are the complete acceptance criteria for user registration?
What are the detailed specifications for dashboard features beyond active users, system status, and project lists?

acceptance_criteria: Given a new user navigates to the registration page, when they provide a valid Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name, then they shall be successfully registered and authenticated.
Given a user is on the login page, when they provide valid email and password credentials, then they shall be successfully authenticated and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given a user is on the login page, when they click "Sign in with Google", then they shall be authenticated via Firebase (Google Sign-in) and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given a user has an active session, when 24 hours pass since their last interaction, then their session token shall expire, requiring re-authentication upon next action.
Given a new user attempts to register, when they submit the registration form with a valid Email, then the system shall accept it as a required field.
Given a new user attempts to register, when they submit the registration form with a valid Full Name, then the system shall accept it as a required field.
Given a new user attempts to register, when they submit the registration form with a valid Role, then the system shall accept it as a required field.
Given a new user attempts to register, when they submit the registration form with a valid Company Name, then the system shall accept it as a required field.
Given a new user attempts to register, when they submit the registration form including an address, then the system shall ignore or not store the address.
Given a new user attempts to register, when they submit the registration form including a telephone number, then the system shall ignore or not store the telephone number.
Given an authenticated user navigates to the project dashboard, then it shall display active user metrics.
Given an authenticated user navigates to the project dashboard, then it shall display the system status.
Given an authenticated user navigates to the project dashboard, then it shall display a list of projects.
Given an attempt to connect to the PostgreSQL database instance from the public internet, then the connection shall be rejected and the instance shall not be publicly exposed.
Given the Cloud Run backend application, when it attempts to connect to the PostgreSQL database, then it shall establish the connection exclusively via Serverless VPC Access.
test_scenarios: Verify successful user registration using email, full name, role, and company name.
Verify successful user login using valid email and password credentials.
Verify successful user login using Google Sign-in.
Verify the project dashboard loads correctly after successful authentication, displaying active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Verify session token expires after 24 hours of inactivity and requires re-authentication.
Verify that attempting to register without providing an email address results in an error message and prevents registration.
Verify that attempting to register without providing a full name results in an error message and prevents registration.
Verify that attempting to register without providing a role results in an error message and prevents registration.
Verify that attempting to register without providing a company name results in an error message and prevents registration.
Verify that providing an address or telephone number during registration does not result in these fields being stored or used.
Verify the PostgreSQL database instance is inaccessible from outside the private VPC.
Verify Cloud Run application establishes its database connection via Serverless VPC Access as intended.
edge_cases: Attempt to register with an email address that is already in use.
Attempt to register with invalid email formats (e.g., missing '@', invalid domain).
Simultaneous login attempts from the same user account from different devices or browsers.
User performs actions immediately before the 24-hour session token expiration, then attempts another action just after.
User attempts to access a protected dashboard feature after their session has expired.
Dashboard displays data correctly when there are no active users, no projects, or the system status is critical/error.
Testing performance under high load conditions to assess if the system remains 'super fast'.
Attempting to register and then immediately logging out and logging back in.
unresolved_behaviors: The definition of "super fast" for system performance is subjective; quantifiable metrics (e.g., specific response times for key actions, page load times) are needed.
The specific acceptance criteria for the overall user registration flow beyond field collection (e.g., email verification, account activation, post-registration onboarding) are not fully detailed.

The detailed features, specific data points, update frequency, and interactivity of 'active user metrics', 'system status', and 'project lists' on the dashboard need to be specified in the next sync meeting.

The specific behaviors for new user registration via Google Sign-in (e.g., how Full Name, Role, Company Name are collected if not provided by Google profile or if they are mandatory post-Google sign-in) are unclear.
The scope and impact of the 'Role' field during user registration are not defined (e.g., does it imply different permissions, access levels, or dashboard views?).

decision_history: The main goal for V1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st, which is a hard constraint due to a marketing campaign launching on September 5th. (D001, D002)
The V1 MVP must include user authentication, a working project dashboard, and the system must be 'super fast'. (D004, D005, D006)
Frontend development will use React. Backend services will use Python with FastAPI, chosen due to existing team libraries. (D007, D008, D009)
PostgreSQL will be used as the project database, utilizing a standard relational model. (D011, D012)
Session management for V1 will use standard PostgreSQL sessions to save budget, with Redis deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker. (D014, D015, D016)

User authentication functionality will not be built in-house. Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1 because it's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches pricing requirements. It will be configured to allow Google sign-in and email logins. (D018, D019, D020, D021, D022)

User registration will require collecting email, full name, role, and company name (as it is a B2B project). Address and telephone number are not required for now. (D025, D026, D027, D028, D029, D030)
The FastAPI backend application will be deployed on Cloud Run, and the React frontend application on Vercel. Vercel deployment is acceptable. (D032, D033, D034)

For backend security, the database must be placed in a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC), and Cloud Run will connect via Serverless VPC Access. The PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet, as backend security is a strict technical constraint. (D036, D037, D038, D039)
The authentication flow needs to be thoroughly tested. (D040)
Session token expiration is set to 24 hours for now, with the possibility to adjust it later. (D042)

The project dashboard must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists. The detailed features of the dashboard will be discussed in the next synchronization meeting. (D044, D045, D046, D047)
Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning. (D050)
dependencies: The project's V1 MVP delivery (September 1st) is a hard dependency for the marketing campaign launch on September 5th.
Firebase authentication integration for development is dependent on Mike provisioning Firebase and providing config keys by July 8th (confirmed).
Dashboard design requires a sync with the marketing team next Wednesday (confirmed).
risks: Marketing advertisements have already been booked, creating a significant external pressure and reinforcing the hard V1 deadline of September 1st (High Likelihood, High Impact if deadline is missed).

The 'super fast' system performance requirement (D006) is not yet quantified, which could lead to scope creep, rework, or missed expectations if not clearly defined (Medium Likelihood, Medium Impact).

Deferring Redis for session management to V2 (D014) poses a risk that standard PostgreSQL sessions may become a performance blocker for V1, requiring last-minute changes or impacting the 'super fast' requirement (Medium Likelihood, Medium Impact).

Security concern regarding Cloud Run for the backend was raised (D035). While mitigation steps (private VPC, Serverless VPC Access, no public exposure) have been decided, any misconfiguration or oversight could have critical impact (Medium Likelihood, High Impact).
alignment_gaps: The precise acceptance criteria for user registration, beyond the required fields (email, full name, role, company name), are not fully specified, potentially leading to differing interpretations during testing or review. (D023)
The 'super fast' system performance requirement is subjective and lacks specific, measurable criteria, which could lead to misalignment on what constitutes 'fast enough'. (D006)

Detailed features and the complete scope for the dashboard are deferred to the next sync (D047), indicating that full stakeholder alignment on this core V1 feature is not yet achieved beyond the high-level metrics.
open_questions: What are the specific, detailed features and the full scope of the project dashboard, beyond active user metrics, system status, and project lists? (Impact: High - essential for V1 scope clarity, requires next sync for resolution).

What are the complete acceptance criteria for user registration, beyond the required fields of email, full name, role, and company name? (Impact: Medium - needed for clear testing and definition of done).
milestones: Phoenix Project V1 MVP must be delivered by September 1st.
Firebase configuration keys (from Mike) must be ready by July 8th.

system_requirements: The Phoenix Project V1 MVP SHALL be delivered by September 1st.
The system SHALL include user authentication functionality for V1.
The system SHALL include a working project dashboard for V1.
The system SHALL be performant, demonstrating 'super fast' response times.
The system SHALL support user registration via email.
The system SHALL support user registration via Google Sign-in.
User registration SHALL collect the user's email address as a required field.
User registration SHALL collect the user's full name as a required field.
User registration SHALL collect the user's role as a required field.
User registration SHALL collect the user's company name as a required field.
User registration SHALL NOT collect the user's address or telephone number for V1.
The authentication flow SHALL be thoroughly tested.
The system SHALL display active user metrics on the project dashboard.
The system SHALL display system status on the project dashboard.
The system SHALL display project lists on the project dashboard.
The system SHALL implement session token expiration with a default duration of 24 hours (adjustable).
data_flows: Frontend (React) to Backend (FastAPI): User interaction data, login requests, project management operations.
Frontend (React) to Firebase Authentication: User authentication requests (e.g., Google Sign-in, Email/Password).
Firebase Authentication to Backend (FastAPI): Authentication tokens or user identity claims (inferred — needs confirmation).
Backend (FastAPI) to PostgreSQL Database: User data, session data, project data, dashboard metrics data.
Backend (FastAPI) (on Cloud Run) to PostgreSQL Database: Secure connection via Serverless VPC Access.
interface_contracts: Firebase Authentication API: Integration for user login (Google Sign-in, Email/Password) and user registration.
Backend API (FastAPI) - User Registration Endpoint: Accepts payload including 'email', 'fullName', 'role', 'companyName'.
Backend API (FastAPI) - Session Management: Expected to handle session creation and validation based on PostgreSQL sessions.
Backend API (FastAPI) - Dashboard Data Endpoints: Expected to provide data for 'active user metrics', 'system status', and 'project lists'.
constraints_and_assumptions: The V1 MVP SHALL be delivered by September 1st, which is a hard constraint due to marketing campaign launch on September 5th.
The frontend SHALL be developed using React.
The backend service SHALL be developed using Python with FastAPI.
The choice of Python for the backend is influenced by existing team libraries (technical bias).
The database SHALL be PostgreSQL using a standard relational model.
Redis for session management SHALL be deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker.
V1 session management SHALL utilize standard PostgreSQL sessions due to budget constraints.
User authentication functionality SHALL NOT be built in-house.
Firebase Authentication SHALL be used for V1 user authentication.
The frontend React application SHALL be deployed on Vercel.
The backend FastAPI application SHALL be deployed on Cloud Run.
The PostgreSQL database instance SHALL be placed in a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
Cloud Run SHALL connect to the database via Serverless VPC Access.
The PostgreSQL instance SHALL NOT be exposed to the public internet.
Backend deployment security is a strict technical constraint.
Marketing advertisements for the project have already been booked, reinforcing the V1 deadline (implies no flexibility on timeline).
open_analysis_questions: What are the detailed acceptance criteria for user registration beyond collected fields?
What are the specific definitions and calculations for 'active user metrics' to be displayed on the dashboard?
What specific details and thresholds constitute 'system status' to be displayed on the dashboard?
What are the complete and detailed features required for the project dashboard (to be discussed in the next sync)?
Who is responsible for the configuration and provisioning of Firebase, ensuring keys are ready by July 8th?
What are the specific details of 'project lists' to be displayed on the dashboard (e.g., content, filtering, sorting)?

stakeholder.business_summary: The team has confirmed the Phoenix Project's V1 Minimum Viable Product (MVP) must launch by September 1st, a hard deadline driven by upcoming marketing campaigns. Key features for this release include user authentication and a functional project dashboard, designed to be 'super fast' for a smooth user experience. We've made core technology choices to ensure timely delivery and a secure system while managing costs.
key_impact: Meeting the September 1st launch deadline is critical to capitalize on pre-booked marketing campaigns and drive initial user adoption.
Users will benefit from a fast and secure platform, featuring essential login capabilities and an initial project overview dashboard.
We're making efficient use of resources by leveraging existing solutions for user management and keeping V1 focused, which helps manage project budget.
Security is a top priority, with technical safeguards in place to protect sensitive data and ensure system integrity.
The platform will gather key B2B information like company names during registration, supporting our business model.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the full details and specific metrics that will be displayed on the project dashboard?
What are the detailed success criteria for new user registration and onboarding?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. There are no hallucinations, and the quality of each view is appropriate for its intended audience. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected.

Yes, we've already booked the ads.

and a working project dashboard.
Let's make sure it's super fast.
we are using React for the frontend
and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.
